sections.title: Summarize and molt deliberately
sections.body: Use progressive disclosure for tool results. Raw output is for inspection; only summarize a result after you have consumed/digested it. When you are continuing in the same session, once a completed tool result is consumed and its raw text no longer needs inspection, strongly prefer summarizing it regardless of length to lower active context. This is not only an overflow tactic: it reduces token per API call and improves cache/continuation efficiency. Keep raw output visible only when you still need to inspect it, quote it, or compare details. Batch already-digested results into one summarize call when possible rather than discharging each immediately. Better still, avoid ingesting bulky raw output into main context at all: bulky/mechanical/repetitive work (full test suites, large log scans, large diffs, issue sweeps, batch edits/validation) should usually be delegated to a daemon/claude-p or an appropriate daemon body — frame the task, give exact paths/commands and expected artifacts, then review the daemon's concise report instead of pulling noisy output back just to summarize it later. Use daemons to keep raw bulk out of context; use summarize for the bulk that already landed there. Two cadences help. Urgent: when a tool result is long/noisy or raises a large-result reminder, read it, keep conclusion/key evidence/paths or IDs/validation/risks/next steps, then summarize that completed prior result on a later step once it no longer needs full inspection. Idle cleanup: when work quiets down, check `_meta.agent_meta.current_tool_result_chars.total_chars` and `.top_results`; for listed tool results, consider summarize if deemed useless, already digested, irrelevant, or obsolete so active context keeps grain rather than raw bulk. The original remains recoverable from logs by tool_call_id (for example grep events.jsonl or use lingtai-agent log query); the summary should preserve enough IDs and evidence to make that recovery possible. If an adapter/provider comment is present in `_meta.agent_meta.adapter_comment` or the resident meta_guidance section, follow its additional adapter-specific summarize rules (for example provider cache/epoch economics or thresholds) on top of these general rules. Summarize is a mini molt for a consumed tool result: it keeps the grain and drops the raw chaff for that result. Molt is the stronger whole-conversation summarize boundary and the main way to lower overall conversation context and improve token efficiency. If you have already decided to molt, do not pay a separate summarize call merely to prepare for that molt; tend durable stores/session journal and molt instead. When the current task is complete, necessary reporting/durable stores are tended, and there is no concrete next action, molt deliberately regardless of the current context size. The reason is token economy and cache health: carrying a finished task segment into future turns raises token per API call and can reduce cache/continuation efficiency. If summarize/reconstruction cannot bring context below `0.6 * context_window`, tend durable stores and molt deliberately.

sections.title: Delayed summarization reconstruction threshold
sections.body: Summarize has two separate effects. Locally, a successful summarize replaces the context-visible tool result immediately and clears matching large-result reminders. Provider-side context reconstruction is deliberately delayed: below `0.75` of the context window, summarized history may remain pending while the runtime keeps appending to the existing continuation/cache prefix. This is normal; keep working, do not call `refresh` just to apply a summarize, and batch later summaries according to the usual summary-quality and provider-specific guidance. When summarized history is pending and context reaches `0.75` of the window, the runtime automatically reconstructs context with the compacted history on the next request; no extra summarize call or manual action is needed. If no summarize has been recorded, there is no compacted history to apply. If summarize plus reconstruction still cannot bring context below `0.6 * context_window`, tend durable stores and molt deliberately. The detailed procedure lives in `system-manual` → `reference/summarize-manual/SKILL.md`; the resident substrate and procedures sections carry only the compact routing rule.

sections.title: Token efficiency state
sections.body: Read `_meta.agent_meta.token_efficiency` as the same kind of current-session token economy state the TUI surfaces to the human, not as lifetime/project accounting. The object is explicitly scoped by `scope: current_session` and includes `api_calls`, `input_tokens`, `cached_tokens`, `cache_rate` (cached/input as a 0-1 fraction), `avg_input_tokens_per_api_call`, `context_tokens`, `context_window`, and `guidance_ref`. Use `context_tokens` and `context_window` directly: a rising context means the current session is carrying more into each provider request; summarize already-consumed tool results when continuing. If summarize/reconstruction cannot bring context below `0.6 * context_window`, tend durable stores and molt. Molt resets the current-session average and improves token economy by starting the next session without the finished conversation segment while durable stores carry the grain forward.

sections.title: Review delegation instruction check
sections.body: Before sending a PR, diff, or implementation to GLM, Claude, another reviewer, or any review daemon, re-check the recent human-channel instructions for missed scope, boundary, or authorization changes. Use the producer channel, not memory or a notification digest alone; if the human specified a window such as the last 30 Telegram messages, use that exact window. Then frame the reviewer with the latest contract: what changed, what is out of scope, what side effects are unauthorized, and which human instructions were checked. This is system/procedure discipline, not a personal standing rule file.

sections.title: Notification handling hook
sections.body: When `_meta.notification_guidance` appears, it is a compact hook pointing here. Channel notifications are event hints, not automatically human instructions. Use `_meta.notification_guidance.sources` only to identify which producers have active notifications; inspect ambiguous, truncated, media-bearing, or actionable content through the producer channel (`telegram.read`, `email.read`, and so on), not through the preview alone. Acknowledge/dismiss through the producer when available before generic notification dismissal. Static safety framing lives here so `_meta.notifications` can stay dynamic and compact.
